topic: Pt-03: XCE teaching opportunities BEYOND code-graph for (1) mcp-coco-index — query expansion via XCE intent-steering pattern, rerank fusion with code-graph HLD/LLD (Phase 001 dependency), ccc_feedback JSONL graduation to active rerank-weight loop, few-shot example-bank retrieval, cross-cutting coco+graph fused rerank stage; and (2) system-spec-kit memory backend / causal graph — semantic trigger matching using existing Voyage-4 1024-dim embeddings, LLM-curated dynamic memory_context assembly, session-trace bounded auto-causal-edge inference within NFR-R01 caps, feedback-ledger.ts driven learned retention/decay (priority-aware basement for constitutional tier, hit-rate-weighted TTL), shared embedding cache + rerank infra between memory and coco-index. 10 iterations, RQ-A1..A5 (coco-index group) + RQ-B1..B5 (memory/causal group). Out of scope: pt-02's 6 open code-graph policy decisions.

researchQuestions.label: Coco-index intent steering + query expansion
researchQuestions.summary: Can XCE's unconditional 'use first' steering pattern + intent classification teach mcp-coco-index when to fire AND how to expand a single user query into intent-tagged multi-queries (e.g. 'show error handling' → ['exception handling', 'try-catch', 'error recovery'])? Examine query.py + SKILL.md.

researchQuestions.label: Coco-index rerank fusion with code-graph HLD/LLD
researchQuestions.summary: Can Phase 001's deterministic HLD/LLD narrative (lib/code-graph-hld-lld.ts) be folded into coco-index's path-class rerank to boost hits near known module boundaries / role anchors? Cross-references seed-resolver.ts + cocoindex-calibration.ts. Hard-depends on Phase 001 ship.

researchQuestions.label: ccc_feedback JSONL graduation to active rerank loop
researchQuestions.summary: Should ccc_feedback's write-only telemetry graduate to a feedback-driven rerank-weight loop? Inspect existing JSONL schema + 026/004 dedup work for precedent. ADAPT design: bounded online learning (reweight vocabulary, no full re-embed).

researchQuestions.label: Few-shot example-bank retrieval for coco-index
researchQuestions.summary: Can prior validated hits surface as positive exemplars in next query (XCE in-context exemplification analog)? Storage / scoring / privacy tradeoffs. ADAPT vs DEFER.

researchQuestions.label: Cross-cutting: coco+graph fused rerank stage
researchQuestions.summary: Should coco-index and code-graph share a single fused rerank stage (one combined score) rather than today's coco→seed-resolver→graph-anchor→re-score pipeline? LOC + risk surface.

researchQuestions.label: Memory backend semantic trigger matching
researchQuestions.summary: Should memory_match_triggers (currently lexical/regex via trigger-matcher.ts) graduate to embedding-based semantic similarity using the existing Voyage-4 1024-dim cache? Precision/recall tradeoff vs. cognitive-tier signal.

researchQuestions.label: Memory backend LLM-curated context assembly
researchQuestions.summary: Can memory_context assembly become LLM-curated (XCE-style dynamic packaging) instead of rule-based templates — e.g. 'first N from causal chain, then 1 exemplar per tier'? Latency + cost tradeoff vs current 4-stage pipeline.

researchQuestions.label: Session-trace bounded causal-edge inference
researchQuestions.summary: Can causal edges be auto-inferred from session traces (queried A → cited B → weak ENABLED edge) within existing NFR-R01 caps (max 0.5 strength, ≤20 edges/node, 100/relation/window)? Examine causal-edges.ts insertEdge + existing createSpecDocumentChain auto-inference.

researchQuestions.label: Feedback-ledger-driven learned retention/decay
researchQuestions.summary: Can lib/feedback/feedback-ledger.ts signals (search_shown, result_cited) drive learned retention decay decisions — priority-aware basement for constitutional tier, hit-rate-weighted TTL? Stay rule-bounded (no online reembed).

researchQuestions.label: Cross-cutting: shared embedding cache + rerank infra (coco + memory)
researchQuestions.summary: Should coco-index and memory backend share embedding cache + rerank stage given both use Voyage? Where does shared infra leak abstractions (coco indexes code chunks, memory indexes spec-doc records)? ADAPT shared layer or DEFER as YAGNI.

qualityGuards.verdict_diversity: ≥1 ADOPT + ≥1 ADAPT + ≥1 DEFER + ≥1 SKIP across all 10 iter findings (or scope-down required)
qualityGuards.cross_phase_dependencies: Each ADOPT/ADAPT must declare dependencies on existing 027 phases (esp. Phase 001 HLD/LLD for RQ-A2)
